title: Exohebea unguicularis
taxonomy: Exohebea unguicularis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is a large and diverse group of plants, comprising over 5,000 species. Exohebea unguicularis is native to the tropical forests of Madagascar and is a member of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. The Apocynaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, each containing a unique set of characteristics and plant species. By understanding the classification of Exohebea unguicularis, scientists can better comprehend its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.

intro: Exohebea unguicularis is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues.
